predictions.name: Filament orientational correlation length
data_sources: Cosmic-web filament catalogues (DisPerSE, Bisous, T-ReX) on SDSS DR16, DESI DR2
predictions.mechanism: Grain-boundary phonon scattering at cosmic-web filaments produces angular correlations on scale r_texture > r* (BAO scale).
predictions.quantitative_form: C_fila(r) = A · exp(-r / r_texture), with r_texture predicted from cascade-stage k_filament: r_texture = r*(z=z_fil) × χ_geom
predictions.discriminator: ΛCDM predicts decorrelation at the BAO scale r* with no excess at larger r; CCDR predicts excess at r_texture > r*. Density-stratified version (CL4 component) further discriminates: same-sign excess in high-density and low-density subsets predicted.
predictions.pass_fail: Pre-register predicted r_texture value (e.g. r_texture = 933 ± 200 Mpc/h) and amplitude A. Test: measured r_texture inside predicted band → PASS; outside → FAIL. No version-tagged recovery allowed.
derivation_recipe: Fix χ_geom and cascade-stage assignment from frozen framework parameters
Compute r* at filament-formation epoch (z ~ 0.5) via CAMB
Compute predicted r_texture and A
Pre-register; no fitting to data permitted

predictions.name: Dark-matter cascade mass tower
predictions.mechanism: Cascade residues from N→N-1→...→4 dimensional reduction form a geometric mass tower {m_k = m_0 · ρ^k} where ρ is the suppression ratio between adjacent cascade stages.
predictions.quantitative_form: Mass-tower: m_k = m_0 · ρ^k for k = 1..N-4. Predicted mass list with uncertainty bands committed before any peak-resolution data is examined.
predictions.discriminator: No competing DM framework predicts a *list of specific masses with geometric ratios*. WIMP, axion, sterile-ν predict either single mass or continuum.
predictions.pass_fail: (NOW partial:) current exclusion curves should NOT exclude any predicted mass at >95% CL; if they do, the corresponding cascade-stage assignment fails. (2030+:) tonne-scale peak resolution finds peaks at predicted masses → PASS; finds peaks at non-predicted masses → FAIL.
derivation_recipe: Derive m_0 from EW crystallisation scale ≈ v_EW
Derive ρ from cascade-stage ratio (geometric, set by χ_k spacing)
Derive N from §4.5 dimension-origin prior (N = 6, 8, 11 candidates)
Output: list [(m_k, σ_m_k)] with full provenance

predictions.name: High-redshift Milgrom acceleration transition to cH₀
predictions.mechanism: Local a₀ → Milgrom from grain-boundary phonon scattering should evolve: at higher z the cosmic horizon scale cH₀(z) is larger and the cascade-residue phonon spectrum samples higher modes, shifting effective a₀ → cH₀(z) at z ≳ 0.3.
predictions.quantitative_form: a₀(z) = a₀(0) · [1 + α_a (z - z_*)] for z > z_*; predicted z_* = 0.3 and α_a derivable from cascade ν
predictions.discriminator: ΛCDM has no a₀ at all (Newtonian limit). Standard MOND has constant a₀. Only CCDR (and a few specific extensions of MOND) predicts z-evolution.
predictions.pass_fail: Stacked KMOS3D + DESI rotation curves at z = 0.6–1.2 give measured a₀(z). PASS if measured a₀ is within ±1σ of predicted; FAIL if outside ±3σ.
derivation_recipe: Compute Milgrom a₀(z=0) from grain-boundary phonon density × ν
Predict z_* from cosmic horizon evolution
Predict α_a from cascade-residue mode spectrum
Output: a₀(z) function, pre-registered

predictions.name: AS-EPRL stage-by-stage parameter agreement
data_sources: No empirical data — pure theory consistency
predictions.mechanism: Asymptotic Safety (continuum) and EPRL spin-foam (combinatorial) computations of cascade-stage parameters γ_AS, γ_EPRL must agree at each stage.
predictions.quantitative_form: |γ_AS(k) - γ_EPRL(k)| / γ_AS(k) < ε(N₄) for each cascade stage k, with ε → 0 as N₄ → ∞
predictions.discriminator: Internal consistency of the framework. No alternative framework predicts this agreement.
predictions.pass_fail: Compute γ_AS via Bahr-Steinhaus stage-by-stage RG. Compute γ_EPRL via spin-foam amplitude. PASS if agreement within 9% (current toy bound); FAIL if disagreement persists at higher N₄.
derivation_recipe: Run continuum AS β-functions at each cascade-stage cutoff
Run EPRL spin-foam amplitudes at matching combinatorial complexity
Compare γ values stage by stage

predictions.name: BH ringdown QNM deviation from Kerr
data_sources: GWOSC GWTC-3 high-SNR ringdown events (PUBLIC): GW190521, GW150914, GW250114
EHT M87* / Sgr A* image data (PUBLIC)
predictions.mechanism: At BH formation, the horizon is a region of χ_4 → 1 boundary; the cascade leaves a residual deformation of the no-hair structure proportional to (χ_4 - 1)·spin.
predictions.quantitative_form: δω_220 / ω_220^Kerr = ε_CCDR · (a_spin) · F(M, η); ε_CCDR predicted from cascade boundary-deformation coefficient
predictions.discriminator: Kerr no-hair predicts δω = 0 exactly. ECOs, fuzzballs, modified gravity predict different functional forms.
predictions.pass_fail: Stack ringdown of all GWTC-3 SNR > 8 events; measure δω_220 population mean. PASS within ±1σ of CCDR; FAIL outside ±3σ. Current upper bound from GW250114 must not be violated.
derivation_recipe: Compute ε_CCDR from boundary-deformation spectrum (P42)
Compute spin and mass dependence F(M, η) from cascade residue at horizon
Pre-register δω(M, η) functional form

predictions.name: Sub-BAO geometric harmonics in matter P(k)
predictions.mechanism: Cascade-stage spacing produces geometric harmonics in the matter power spectrum at sub-BAO scales (k > k_BAO) with k ratios determined by cascade-stage suppression ρ.
predictions.quantitative_form: P(k_n) / P_{ΛCDM}(k_n) = 1 + A_n cos(2π k_n / k_*) for k_n = k_* · ρ^n; A_n, k_*, ρ predicted
predictions.discriminator: ΛCDM has smooth sub-BAO P(k). CCDR predicts specific geometric peaks. Other frameworks (interacting DM, etc.) predict different peak structures.
predictions.pass_fail: Measure 1D P_F(k) deviation from smooth ΛCDM at k = k_1, k_2, k_3 (predicted positions). PASS within ±1σ of predicted A_n at each; FAIL if peaks absent at >3σ.
derivation_recipe: Compute k_* from cascade-stage transition scale
Compute ρ from frozen geometric ratio
Predict (k_n, A_n) sequence
Pre-register and test against Lyα data

predictions.name: Void-wall transverse kurtosis k₄ > 4
data_sources: REVOLVER / VIDE void catalogues on SDSS/DESI (PUBLIC)
ZOBOV / DESI Y3 void catalogue (when released)
predictions.mechanism: Grain-boundary phonon scattering at void walls produces non-Gaussian density profile tails with predicted transverse kurtosis k₄ > 4 (above Gaussian k₄ = 3).
predictions.quantitative_form: k₄(transverse radial-drift) = 3 + δk₄, with δk₄ ≈ 1.0–1.5 predicted from grain-boundary scattering amplitude × ν
predictions.discriminator: ΛCDM predicts k₄ = 3 (Gaussian) for void walls; CCDR predicts k₄ > 4. Alternative MOND microfoundings give k₄ = 3.
predictions.pass_fail: Stack void walls in catalogue; compute k₄ of transverse radial-drift distribution. PASS if k₄ > 4 at >2σ; FAIL if k₄ ≤ 3.5 at >2σ.
derivation_recipe: Compute grain-boundary scattering amplitude from ν and grain-size r_grain
Predict δk₄ from scattering kernel convolution
Pre-register predicted k₄ value

predictions.name: Secular w(z) drift from bulk RVM cooling
data_sources: DESI DR3 (2027), Euclid Q3, LSST Y1 (decisive)
predictions.mechanism: Projected bulk vacuum density C₀(t) drifts at rate ∝ ν_bulk, inducing time-dependent effective ρ_Λ in our 3+1D slice.
predictions.quantitative_form: w(z) - (-1) = -ν_bulk · (1+z)^β; ν_bulk and β must be frozen before any w(z) fit
predictions.discriminator: ΛCDM: flat w = -1. Quintessence: w(z) increases with z (opposite sign). CCDR-evaporation: w(z) decreases with z. Distinguishable from both.
predictions.pass_fail: (NOW:) Joint DESI DR2 + Pantheon+ w(z) reconstruction must show -ν_bulk · (1+z)^β within ±2σ. (2027:) DESI DR3 + Euclid Q3 decisive at ±0.5σ level.
derivation_recipe: Derive ν_bulk from bulk-RVM coefficient — currently a free parameter; must be committed or have a prior
Derive β from bulk-geometry cooling-profile structure
Output: w(z) prediction with bands
Note: framework should either freeze ν_bulk now or pre-register a prior; otherwise this collapses into fitting

predictions.name: Bulk-Weyl CMB B-mode component with distinct ℓ-shape
predictions.mechanism: Bulk Weyl-squared term W_5 sources tensor B-modes at the 5→4 reduction time, leaving an imprint at large angular scales with ℓ-shape distinct from inflationary tensor B-modes.
predictions.quantitative_form: C_ℓ^BB(total) = C_ℓ^BB(inflation) + A_BW · f_BW(ℓ); f_BW(ℓ) predicted from bulk-Weyl mode structure at trigger; A_BW has theoretical uncertainty O(1)
predictions.discriminator: Inflationary B-mode: reionization bump at ℓ ≈ 5, recombination bump at ℓ ≈ 80. Bulk-Weyl B-mode: distinct ℓ-shape from horizon-entry at trigger. Shapes are orthogonal templates.
predictions.pass_fail: (NOW:) BK18 bounds A_BW · ⟨f_BW(ℓ_BK)⟩ < 10⁻³ in Planck units → constrains framework's bulk geometry. (2029:) LiteBIRD detection at predicted ℓ-shape → PASS; absence at ℓ-shape sensitivity → constrains but doesn't falsify (OP15).
derivation_recipe: Derive f_BW(ℓ) from bulk Weyl mode at 5→4 reduction surface
Derive A_BW lower bound from cascade trigger amplitude
Pre-register ℓ-shape template (template = the prediction; amplitude has uncertainty)

predictions.name: b→sμμ cross-channel sign-coherence with zero C₁₀ shift
data_sources: LHCb full Run 1+2+3 b→sμμ angular results (PUBLIC, arXiv:2512.18053 and subsequent)
predictions.mechanism: TeV-scale cascade-residue lattice/grain-boundary remnant produces a real, vector-like, CP-even shift in C_9^eff with zero shift in C_10, scalar, tensor, or CP-asymmetric coefficients.
frozen_parameters_required: sign and approximate magnitude of δC_9
predictions.quantitative_form: δC_9^eff > 0 (real, vector, CP-even), δC_10 = 0, δC_S = δC_T = δC_9' = 0; specific same-sign pattern across R_K, R_K*, B_s→φμμ, P_5' in adjacent q² bins
predictions.discriminator: Z' explanations: typically affect C_10 too. Leptoquark: scalar/tensor pieces. CCDR-specific: vector real CP-even only. Cross-channel sign coherence with zero in predicted-zero observables is the signature.
predictions.pass_fail: Multi-channel global fit. PASS if (δC_9 > 0) ∧ (|δC_10|/|δC_9| < 0.2) ∧ (cross-channel sign coherence > 90%); FAIL if C_10 shift comparable to C_9, or scalar/tensor pieces non-zero at >3σ.
derivation_recipe: Specify which Wilson coefficients should be non-zero in the cascade-residue model
Specify cross-channel sign relations
Compute χ² of CCDR pattern vs alternatives on public LHCb data

predictions.name: Boundary-deformation residual spectrum
predictions.mechanism: Compactification of higher-D bulk into 3+1D slice leaves boundary-deformation residuals scaling with spin, tidal field, accretion environment, Weyl curvature, and (χ_k - 1).
frozen_parameters_required: scaling exponents (α_J, α_T, α_W) for spin, tide, Weyl
predictions.quantitative_form: |ε_ℓm(k)| = c_dim · J^{α_J} · T^{α_T} · W^{α_W} · (χ_k - 1)^{α_χ}; specific scaling exponents predicted
predictions.discriminator: Kerr / GR: ε = 0. ECOs / fuzzballs / boson stars: specific stochastic spectra. CCDR: ε scales with environment-correlated combinations.
predictions.pass_fail: After subtracting known astrophysical effects, residual ε_ℓm should (a) vanish in clean Schwarzschild ≈ 0, (b) scale predictably with spin. PASS if scaling consistent with predicted exponents; FAIL if scaling random or wrong direction.
derivation_recipe: Derive scaling exponents from cascade boundary-deformation theory
Compute predicted residual spectrum
Compare to EHT shadow circularity + GW ringdown population statistics

predictions.name: Joint density-stratification of P3 and P38 same-sign
data_sources: Cosmic-web filament + VAST void catalogues on SAME survey (DESI DR2)
predictions.mechanism: Both P3 (filament texture) and P38 (void-wall kurtosis) derive from grain-boundary scattering; their density-stratified signatures must share sign.
predictions.quantitative_form: sign(Δr_texture^high − Δr_texture^low) · sign(Δk_4^high − Δk_4^low) > 0
predictions.discriminator: If P3 and P38 came from different mechanisms, their density splits could have opposite signs. Same-sign requires shared underlying mechanism.
predictions.pass_fail: Compute density-quantile splits independently on filament and void catalogues. PASS if same sign at >2σ each; FAIL if opposite sign at >2σ.
derivation_recipe: Standard density-quantile stratification on each catalogue
Sign test on combined statistic

predictions.name: Dark photon kinetic mixing ε ~ ν
predictions.mechanism: If optical-sector U(1) exists in cascade, kinetic mixing with SM photon has ε ~ ν.
predictions.quantitative_form: ε_dark = c_mix · ν, with c_mix predicted O(1); for ν = 5×10⁻³, ε ≈ 10⁻³ in mass-range ~ 0.1–10 GeV
predictions.discriminator: Standard dark photon: ε is free. CCDR: ε is tied to ν, which is independently measured.
predictions.pass_fail: If experimental upper limits on ε for any mass in predicted range exclude ε ~ ν, that combination is falsified. If ε at predicted value detected, PASS.
derivation_recipe: Use frozen ν
Compute c_mix from cascade U(1) sector
Compare to public dark-photon exclusion plots

predictions.name: Cosmic-string tension Gμ ~ 10⁻⁶ in PTA window
predictions.mechanism: Edge-dislocation line defects of spacetime lattice yield cosmic strings with predicted tension μ ~ 10⁻⁶ M_Pl² / α.
predictions.quantitative_form: Gμ ~ 10⁻⁶ ± half a decade
predictions.discriminator: ΛCDM has no preferred string tension. CCDR predicts a value tied to cascade structure.
predictions.pass_fail: NANOGrav PTA spectrum is consistent with Gμ in [10⁻⁷, 3·10⁻⁶] from public constraints. PASS if any cosmic-string fit within band; FAIL if PTA spectrum cleanly fit by SMBHB only with strings disfavoured at >3σ in predicted band.
derivation_recipe: Standard cosmic-string h_c(f) template
Cross-fit against NANOGrav 15-yr posterior

predictions.name: CDT simplex chirality count = δ_CP
predictions.mechanism: Simplicial orientation asymmetry in CDT C-phase yields CP phase δ via combinatorial count.
predictions.quantitative_form: δ_CP = arctan(N_+ - N_-) / (N_+ + N_-) where N_± are positively/negatively oriented 4-simplices, computed from CDT ensemble
predictions.discriminator: SM has δ_CP as free parameter. CCDR computes it from CDT geometry.
predictions.pass_fail: Run CDT-plusplus ensemble; count chiral asymmetry. PASS if computed δ_CP matches PDG (1.144 ± 0.027 rad) within 20%; FAIL if outside ±50%.
derivation_recipe: Build CDT-plusplus, generate C-phase ensemble at N_4 ~ 10^4
Count chirality asymmetry per configuration
Average over Markov chain

predictions.name: DA/O upper bound N ≤ 11 on cascade depth
data_sources: Theoretical bound from division-algebra framework + observational cosmology
predictions.mechanism: C ⊗ H ⊗ O division-algebra structure constrains total cascade stage count.
predictions.discriminator: Most frameworks have no constraint on cascade depth. CCDR predicts N ≤ 11.
predictions.pass_fail: PASS if all empirical N inference from cascade structure ≤ 11; FAIL if any inference requires N > 11.
derivation_recipe: Verify C ⊗ H ⊗ O dimension count enforces N ≤ 11

predictions.name: Axion mass in 10⁻⁵–10⁻² eV window
predictions.mechanism: PQ-scale crystallisation Goldstone with mass set by QCD instanton potential.
predictions.quantitative_form: m_a ∈ [10⁻⁵, 10⁻²] eV with strongest probability near 10⁻⁴–10⁻³ eV
predictions.discriminator: Other DM frameworks predict either no axion or different mass range. CCDR-AQN: specific window tied to QCD-stage crystallisation.
predictions.pass_fail: PASS if axion detected in window with predicted g_aγγ coupling; FAIL if window comprehensively excluded by ADMX + HAYSTAC + future experiments.
derivation_recipe: Compute f_a from QCD-stage crystallisation
Predict m_a = m_π · f_π / f_a

predictions.name: Right-handed neutrino at 10⁹–10¹⁵ GeV
predictions.mechanism: Crystal-boundary mode with Majorana mass on de Sitter / BH horizons.
predictions.quantitative_form: M_R ∈ [10⁹, 10¹⁵] GeV with see-saw implication m_ν · M_R ~ v_EW²
predictions.discriminator: SM: no νR. Type-I see-saw: M_R free. CCDR: M_R tied to cascade-stage boundary energy.
predictions.pass_fail: PASS if 0νββ rate consistent with predicted M_R; FAIL if 0νββ excluded at level requiring M_R outside predicted range.
derivation_recipe: Compute crystal-boundary energy from cascade structure
Predict M_R, hence m_ν via see-saw

predictions.name: Joint multi-channel consistency (P39+P40+P41+P42)
predictions.mechanism: All four predictions arise from same lattice/bulk-Weyl/cascade structure. Joint posterior in (ν_bulk, c_W, lattice scale, boundary-deformation amplitude) space must be self-consistent.
predictions.quantitative_form: Joint posterior support region must be non-empty in (ν_bulk, c_W × C_5²/G_5, δC_9, ε_ℓm) space.
predictions.discriminator: If P39 + P40 + P41 + P42 came from independent mechanisms, joint constraint would be a coincidence. Same mechanism predicts overlapping support.
predictions.pass_fail: Compute joint posterior. PASS if 68% support region non-empty AND consistent across independent measurements; FAIL if joint posterior is empty (mutual inconsistency).
derivation_recipe: Combine per-channel likelihoods
Compute joint posterior
Check overlap

predictions.name: RVM BAO scale shift δr*/r* = ν/2
predictions.mechanism: RVM running of vacuum density shifts r* by ν/2 at matter-radiation equality.
predictions.quantitative_form: δr*/r* = ν/2
predictions.discriminator: ΛCDM: δr* = 0. RVM-class models: predict ν/2 shift.
predictions.pass_fail: Once ν frozen, measure r* shift in DESI DR2/DR3. PASS within ±1σ; FAIL outside ±3σ.
derivation_recipe: Commit ν
Compute δr*/r*
Compare to DESI
predictions.repair_required: Resolve OP11 (ν 500× discrepancy) by either committing to joint or standalone extractor with documented justification, OR registering ν as uncertain parameter with prior

predictions.name: Filament non-Gaussian bispectrum f_NL^grain
predictions.mechanism: Grain-boundary scattering imprints scale-dependent f_NL on filament network.
predictions.quantitative_form: f_NL^grain ~ (r*/r_grain)² × 10⁻²
predictions.discriminator: ΛCDM: f_NL_local ≈ 0. CCDR: scale-dependent shape on filaments.
predictions.pass_fail: PASS if f_NL_grain measurement within ±1σ of predicted; FAIL outside ±3σ.
derivation_recipe: Commit r_grain
Compute f_NL^grain
Compare to Planck/Euclid
predictions.repair_required: Commit r_grain (grain size scale) as frozen parameter

predictions.name: QGP viscosity enhancement at phase boundaries
data_sources: ALICE / CMS QGP η/s public results
predictions.mechanism: Phase-boundary scattering enhances η/s above KSS bound by specific factor.
predictions.quantitative_form: η/s = (1/4π) · (1 + c_η/s · ν); c_η/s predicted
predictions.discriminator: AdS/CFT: η/s = 1/4π. CCDR: enhanced by c_η/s · ν.
predictions.pass_fail: PASS if measured enhancement matches predicted within ±1σ.
derivation_recipe: Compute c_η/s from phase-boundary scattering
Use frozen ν
Compare to ALICE/CMS
predictions.repair_required: Compute c_η/s from cascade phase-boundary mechanism

predictions.name: PTA GW spectral index shift δn_GW ~ ν/3
predictions.mechanism: Cascade-residue stochastic GW background contributes to PTA signal with specific spectral index shift.
predictions.quantitative_form: δn_GW = -ν/3 (relative to standard SMBHB α = -2/3)
predictions.discriminator: SMBHB-only: n_GW ≈ -2/3. CCDR: small shift to -2/3 - ν/3.
predictions.pass_fail: Measure spectral index in NANOGrav 15-yr posterior. PASS if shift matches; FAIL if opposite sign or magnitude > 3× predicted.
derivation_recipe: Commit ν
Compute δn_GW
Compare to NG15 spectral fit

predictions.name: PTA × cosmic-web κ correlation amplitude
predictions.mechanism: Reducing-volume effect at pulsar positions correlates PTA residuals with local lensing convergence κ.
predictions.quantitative_form: Pearson correlation r(WRMS, κ) = r_predicted; r_predicted ≈ ?? (NEEDS COMMIT)
predictions.discriminator: Null hypothesis: r = 0. CCDR predicts specific r value.
predictions.pass_fail: PASS if measured r within ±1σ of predicted; FAIL outside ±3σ. Currently r = +0.266 → must be in predicted band.
derivation_recipe: Compute expected r from reducing-volume mechanism + pulsar timing model
Pre-register
predictions.repair_required: Compute predicted r amplitude (currently only sign is predicted)

predictions.name: Frozen-vs-live DM fraction in growth fσ_8
predictions.mechanism: Cascade history produces mix of frozen + live DM components affecting growth.
predictions.quantitative_form: fσ_8(z) = fσ_8^ΛCDM(z) · (1 + α · f_live · (1+z)^γ); f_live ≈ 0.154 predicted at present
predictions.discriminator: ΛCDM: standard growth. CCDR: specific deficit at low z.
predictions.pass_fail: Joint fit of fσ_8(z) data. PASS if α and f_live within ±1σ; FAIL outside ±3σ.
derivation_recipe: Derive f_live from cascade-live retention fraction
Compute α
Test against fσ_8
predictions.repair_required: Derive f_live and α from first principles rather than fitting

predictions.name: Density-correlated Ω_DM/Ω_B in lensing convergence
predictions.mechanism: Cascade history depends on local density; ratio Ω_DM/Ω_B varies with environment, traced by κ on density-stratified subsamples.
predictions.quantitative_form: Δκ(high − low density) = c_κ · ν; c_κ predicted from cascade-residue ratio
predictions.discriminator: ΛCDM: Δκ tracks pure density. CCDR: extra component proportional to ν.
predictions.pass_fail: Measure Δκ on density-stratified ACT DR6 patches. PASS if Δκ within predicted ±1σ; FAIL outside ±3σ.
derivation_recipe: Compute c_κ from cascade-residue cross-section
Pre-register

predictions.name: Staged CMB spectral distortions μ, y from cascade stages
predictions.mechanism: Each cascade stage at z > 10⁶ injects energy contributing to μ and y.
predictions.quantitative_form: μ = Σ_k μ_k(ΔE_k), y = Σ_k y_k(ΔE_k); predicted total μ, y
predictions.discriminator: ΛCDM Silk damping: standard small μ, y. CCDR: additional contribution from cascade stages.
predictions.pass_fail: (NOW:) Predicted (μ, y) must be within FIRAS upper limits. (2030+:) PIXIE measurement of μ within ±1σ of predicted.
derivation_recipe: Compute ΔE_k per cascade stage at z > 10⁶
Compute resulting μ, y
Compare to FIRAS
predictions.repair_required: Compute per-stage energy injection from cascade rate

predictions.name: Density-correlated BAO sound horizon
predictions.mechanism: Cascade history influences r_d in a density-stratified way.
predictions.quantitative_form: δr_d/r_d (high − low density) = c_r · ν · (1 + δ_8); c_r predicted
predictions.discriminator: ΛCDM: δr_d = 0. CCDR: specific sign+magnitude.
predictions.pass_fail: (NOW:) Round 7 sign-inversion shows -0.186 on proxy. Must be resolved by deriving predicted sign from cascade.
derivation_recipe: Derive sign of δr_d/r_d from cascade-history mechanism
Resolve OP12

predictions.mechanism: ν is the RVM running coefficient at TGFT condensate point.
predictions.quantitative_form: ν = ?? (REPAIR — OP11 unresolved)

predictions.name: Optical-phonon DM mass + cross-section
predictions.mechanism: Gapped scalar excitation of lattice basis with mass ~ EW crystallisation scale.
predictions.quantitative_form: m_DM = 100 ± ?? GeV; σ_DM ≈ ν²(ℏ/m_DM c)² ~ 10⁻⁴² m²
predictions.discriminator: Standard WIMP: m, σ free. CCDR: tied to ν and EW crystallisation.
predictions.pass_fail: PASS if direct detection finds peak at predicted mass with cross-section in band; FAIL if region excluded at >95% CL.

Derive σ_DM

predictions.name: Koide Q = 2/3 charged-lepton mass relation
predictions.mechanism: Empirical fact: Q = (m_e + m_μ + m_τ)/(√m_e + √m_μ + √m_τ)² ≈ 0.667 ≈ 2/3 to <0.1%. Framework asserts this is theorem of hexagonal C₆ᵥ BZ symmetry (not yet proven rigorously).
predictions.quantitative_form: Q_measured = 0.66666 ± 0.00007 (from PDG); Q_predicted = 2/3 = 0.66667
predictions.discriminator: SM: Q is accidental coincidence. CCDR: Q = 2/3 exactly from hex BZ.
predictions.pass_fail: Observational: Q is measured equal to 2/3 to within 0.1% — consistency fact. NOT a test of derivation; the derivation itself requires §21 execution.
predictions.derivation_complexity: high (theorem proof) — currently asserted, not proven
derivation_recipe: Prove Q = 2/3 follows rigorously from C₆ᵥ symmetry on Hermitian mass matrix
(Independent of §21 lattice execution.)
predictions.note: Test the THEOREM (does C₆ᵥ symmetry actually force Q = 2/3?) rather than the observational fact.
